message:
fix(indexer): validate shard-build and full-set invariants before publishing lexical rebuild

Harden the staged-shard rebuild path against silently publishing a
broken lexical index. Two new validation points sit between "shard
finished building" and "commit the artifact":

1. Per-shard validation at build completion, via
   `validate_lexical_rebuild_shard_build_result`:
   - Re-open the freshly built shard index with `live_tantivy_doc_count`.
     If it returns `None`, the shard is not searchable on disk — that
     previously would have still been pushed onto the publish list.
     Now it's a hard error naming the shard index path.
   - Cross-check the indexer's reported `indexed_docs` against what a
     fresh Tantivy reader sees. If they disagree, fail with both numbers
     in the message (typical cause: commit/segment mismatch after a
     concurrent writer touched the same shard dir).
   - Cross-check the observed doc count against the shard plan's
     `message_count`. If the planner expected N messages but we indexed
     M, surface the delta so the next run's planner logs can correlate
     with the failure.
   The helper returns a `LexicalRebuildShardMergeArtifact` on success;
   the hot loop now pushes the validated artifact instead of
   constructing one inline, so a shard that fails validation never
   appears in the publish list and `processed_conversations` /
   `indexed_docs` counters are only bumped after validation passes.

2. Full-set validation just before publish, via
   `validate_complete_lexical_rebuild_shard_artifacts`:
   - Cardinality check: `artifacts.len() == shard_plan.shards.len()`
     — catches any case where a retry path dropped a shard.
   - Per-shard ordering check: `artifact.{first,last}_shard_index ==
     plan.shard_index` — the publish path treats the artifact order as
     identity, so a reordered artifact would silently mis-merge.
   - Per-shard live-reader check on each artifact path (guarding
     against a shard that was valid at build time but got clobbered
     between build completion and publish — e.g. another writer
     removing the directory).
   - Per-shard doc-count check against the plan, same semantics as
     above.
   Called right after the existing artifact-count precondition in the
   publish block so all three invariants (count, order, readability,
   doc-parity) are checked before any commit happens.

Also contains a rustfmt cleanup in `tests/golden_robot_docs.rs`: the
one-line `assert_golden` call for the `exit-codes` topic splits
across lines to fit the 100-col rule.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
